title: Design waterfall process for a government compliance project
prompt: We need to deliver a tax reporting platform for a federal agency. The project must comply with FISMA and Section 508 accessibility standards. There are 6 stakeholder groups, a 14-month timeline, and a fixed-price contract. Design a complete waterfall phase-gate structure with entry/exit criteria for each gate that accounts for the regulatory compliance checkpoints.
expected_behavior: Defines all 7 standard waterfall phases from initiation through close-out
Includes compliance-specific gates or checkpoints for FISMA and Section 508
Specifies measurable entry and exit criteria per gate
Accounts for fixed-price contract constraints in gate approvals
Produces a lifecycle diagram and gate calendar
Assigns gate review roles with separation of duties

title: Establish requirements baseline for 200+ requirements
prompt: Our ERP modernization project has collected 217 requirements from 4 business units. We need to organize them into a formal requirements baseline with full traceability before we can proceed to the design phase. Many requirements overlap or conflict across business units, and 38 are marked as non-functional. Build the requirements baseline including the traceability matrix structure and signoff process.
expected_behavior: Classifies all requirements as functional, non-functional, constraint, or assumption
Detects and resolves overlapping or conflicting requirements across business units
Creates a traceability matrix linking requirements to source, design, and test
Defines the formal signoff protocol with designated approvers per business unit
Establishes the change control process for post-baseline modifications
Prioritizes using MoSCoW or equivalent method

title: Gate review where 2 of 5 exit criteria are not met
prompt: We are at the Gate 3 review (end of Design phase) for our banking platform migration. The 5 exit criteria are: (1) Architecture document approved, (2) Database schema reviewed, (3) API specifications complete, (4) Security threat model validated, (5) Performance benchmarks established. Criteria 4 and 5 are not met — the security review is pending external auditor availability and performance benchmarks were deprioritized. Conduct the gate review and provide your recommendation.
expected_behavior: Scores each of the 5 exit criteria as met, partially met, or not met
Provides specific evidence references for met criteria
Assesses severity and downstream impact of the 2 unmet criteria
Does NOT recommend unconditional gate approval with unmet security criteria
Recommends conditional approval or rejection with remediation plan
Documents the gate decision with conditions and deadlines

title: Change control for a scope addition mid-design phase
prompt: We are midway through the Design phase of our supply chain management system. The VP of Operations has requested adding real-time IoT sensor integration for warehouse temperature monitoring — this was not in the approved requirements baseline. The addition would affect the architecture, add 3 new API endpoints, require a new vendor integration, and potentially extend the timeline by 6 weeks. Process this change request through formal change control.
expected_behavior: Creates a formal change request with all required fields populated
Conducts impact analysis across scope, schedule, cost, risk, and resources
Quantifies the 6-week schedule impact and vendor integration risk
Classifies the change as major magnitude requiring full CCB review
Identifies all affected baseline documents that would need updating
Provides a clear CCB recommendation with conditions for approval
